name: Navarredonda Pine Forest Trail
name_original: Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda (PR-AV 19) — circular desde Parador de Gredos
description: Hike the 5.0-mile Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda (PR-AV 19) circular route from Parador de Gredos through ancient Scots pine forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Navarredonda De Gredos offers access to the Sierra de Gredos mountain range, characterized by its mountainous terrain. The region features ancient glacial valleys, serene lagoons, and imposing cliffs, with Pico Almanzor as its highest peak. Extensive Scots pine forests provide shaded paths, and the Tormes River flows through the area, creating natural pools and streams. Best hiking trails around Navarredonda De Gredos The most popular hiking route is El Valle de Gredos — circular por el río Tormes, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Tormes River, creating a scenic landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Navarredonda Pine Forest Trail, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path. This trail leads through extensive Scots pine forests. Local hikers also love the Senda del Puerto del Arenal (PR-AV 45) from Navarredonda de Gredos — round trip in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, an 11.1 miles (17.9 km) trail leading through the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, often completed in about 5 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Navarredonda De Gredos is defined by glacial valleys, pine forests, and the Tormes River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging treks. The routes in Navarredonda De Gredos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Navarredonda De Gredos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: Navarredonda de Gredos offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking routes. These trails cater to various skill levels, from leisurely walks through pine forests to more challenging treks into the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: The trails in Navarredonda de Gredos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning mountainous terrain, the serene glacial lagoons, and the extensive Scots pine forests that provide shaded paths.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Navarredonda de Gredos has several easy trails perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343319947/el-valle-de-gredos-circular-por-el-rio-tormes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Valle de Gredos — circular por el río Tormes&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km route that follows the scenic Tormes River. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Puerto del Arenal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the area are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343319947/el-valle-de-gredos-circular-por-el-rio-tormes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Valle de Gredos — circular por el río Tormes&lt;/a&gt; along the Tormes River, or the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19433798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Navarredonda de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced hikers, the region offers more demanding routes that venture deeper into the Sierra de Gredos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380930436/senda-del-puerto-del-arenal-pr-av-45-from-navarredonda-de-gredos-round-trip-in-the-parque-regional-de-la-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Puerto del Arenal (PR-AV 45)&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 17.9 km round trip through the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, offering significant elevation gain and panoramic views.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see on a hike?
A: The Sierra de Gredos is rich in dramatic landscapes. You can find stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, and explore impressive mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2684507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenera Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1711130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass (1,663 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its glacial formations, including the iconic Circo and Laguna Grande de Gredos.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the area around Navarredonda de Gredos features historical sites. You can explore castles such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3986954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6297961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Constable Dávalos&lt;/a&gt;. The charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5711389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mombeltrán&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: The Sierra de Gredos offers great hiking opportunities throughout the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is ideal for enjoying the natural pools and streams of the Tormes River, while winter offers a unique experience for those prepared for colder, potentially snowy conditions in higher altitudes.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or cooling off during a hike?
A: Yes, the Tormes River originates within the municipality and flows through the area, creating natural pools and clear streams. These spots are perfect for cooling off, especially during the warmer months. The Tamborríos recreational area also provides a pleasant spot for relaxation amidst the pine forests.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The Sierra de Gredos is a designated nature reserve, home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for the Iberian ibex, golden eagles, and Eurasian black vultures. The region also boasts unique plant species like the Gredos violet and Spanish moonwort.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking areas?
A: While the area is generally accessible, public transport options directly to trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Navarredonda de Gredos and plan your routes accordingly, as many trails originate directly from the town or nearby accessible points.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: Navarredonda de Gredos and its surrounding areas typically offer designated parking spots near popular trailheads and recreational areas. For specific routes, it's recommended to check the route details on komoot for information on parking availability at the starting point.
tips:
Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda (PR-AV 19) is a short (7 km) but beautiful route that connects the Parador de Gredos parking lot with the town of Navarredonda. Pass through a beautiful mature pine forest of Scots pine (Pinus sylvestris).

It is of low difficulty, even cyclable throughout its entire length, without a notable gradient. Recommended throughout the year and for the whole family.
Small town at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos and very close to Río Tormes. The Church of Navarredonda de Gredos is one of the most important elements of the nucleus.
Right at the entrance to the town of Navarredonda de Gredos is its stone church, which is one of the most important elements of the town.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda (PR-AV 19) and how long does it take?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of low difficulty, making it suitable for all fitness levels and the entire family. It features an 'inappreciable' gradient, meaning there are no significant uphill or downhill sections. The route is approximately 8 kilometers (5 miles) long and typically takes between 1 hour 15 minutes and 2 hours 11 minutes to complete, depending on your pace.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is circular and starts from the Parador de Gredos. You can find parking available at the Parador de Gredos parking lot, which serves as a convenient starting point for the hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily winds through a beautiful, mature Scots pine forest, known for its distinctive reddish-orange bark. You'll encounter both old-growth pines, some over a century old, and younger trees. The path itself varies, consisting of unpaved sections, footpaths, and some paved areas. As it descends gently into the forest, you'll follow a zigzagging path, occasionally finding old stone benches for rest.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda is a dog-friendly trail. However, as it is located within the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, it is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda is recommended year-round. Its low difficulty and sheltered pine forest make it enjoyable in various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful colors, while the dense pine canopy can provide shade in summer and a magical atmosphere in winter.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll walk through the Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda de Gredos itself, a beautiful pine forest. Other features include the Fuente del Rey (King's Fountain) spring, which feeds a small stream, and further along, the Peña Histórica (Historical Rock) with a cross on a granite boulder. From the Prado de Navahondilla meadow, there are expansive views of the surroundings. You'll also pass near the Navarredonda de Gredos Church.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda within the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with other trails in the area. Notably, it covers parts of the 'A vueltas con Gredos' and 'A vueltas con Gredos-MTB' routes, as well as a smaller portion of the 'Camino del Prado Viejo'.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I see?
faqs.answer: The Scots pine forest is teeming with life. You'll be walking among ancient pines, some over a century old. The scenery also includes small clearings with wild roses and broom, and damper, shadier areas with ferns. Interpretative panels along the trail provide information about the local flora and forest birds, encouraging observation of the rich ecosystem.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, since the trail connects the Parador de Gredos parking lot with the town of Navarredonda de Gredos, you'll find options for cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the town itself. The Parador de Gredos also offers dining facilities.

waypoints.name: Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda de Gredos
